scope.summary:
Goal: Add two Obsidian-core wikilink behaviors on top of the MT-015 wikilink/transclusion engine in the native (egui) rich-text editor: (1) CREATE-FROM-UNRESOLVED — clicking an unresolved `[[Title]]` link offers a 'Create note "Title"' affordance that POSTs a new knowledge document via the MT-037 backend binding and then rewrites the in-document mark from unresolved to resolved; and (2) ALIAS RESOLUTION — resolve a wikilink target not only by exact title/ref but also against a per-document `aliases` list, and surface alias matches in the MT-015 autocomplete dropdown alongside exact-title matches. This MT is pure frontend logic layered on existing panes and existing backend APIs — NO backend rewrite. Backend gaps are TYPED BLOCKERS, not new endpoints.

This MT does NOT re-implement the wikilink mark, the `[[...]]` parser, or the autocomplete UI shell — those already exist from MT-015 (E2-MT015-WikilinksTransclusion). This MT adds: a richer resolver (resolver.rs) and the alias-aware autocomplete data path (autocomplete.rs) feeding the existing MT-015 dropdown widget.

Files to CREATE (new modules under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/):
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/wikilinks/resolver.rs  — the resolution engine. Define:
      * enum WikilinkResolution { Resolved { document_id: String, matched_by: MatchKind }, Unresolved { title: String } }
      * enum MatchKind { ExactRef, ExactTitle, Alias { alias: String } }
      * struct ResolverIndex { by_title: HashMap<String, String /*doc_id*/>, by_alias: HashMap<String, String /*doc_id*/>, aliases_supported: bool }
      * fn resolve_wikilink(index: &ResolverIndex, target: &str) -> WikilinkResolution — normalize target (trim, case-insensitive per Obsidian default), check exact ref/title first, then alias map; if no match return Unresolved.
      * fn create_note_intent(title: &str) -> EditorIntent::CreateNote { title } — emit a command-bus intent (reuse the WP-011 command bus via command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) rather than calling the network inline from the click handler.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/wikilinks/autocomplete.rs  — alias-aware candidate provider. Define:
      * struct WikilinkCandidate { document_id: String, display_title: String, matched_alias: Option<String>, score: i64 }
      * fn candidates_for_query(index: &ResolverIndex, query: &str) -> Vec<WikilinkCandidate> — fuzzy-match query against both titles and aliases, dedupe by document_id (prefer the higher score; if matched by alias, set matched_alias so the dropdown can render 'Title (alias: "foo")'), sort by score desc.
      This module feeds the EXISTING MT-015 autocomplete dropdown — do not build a new dropdown widget; produce the candidate list the MT-015 widget consumes.

Files to EDIT (extend, do not rewrite):
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/wikilinks/mod.rs  — add `pub mod resolver;` and `pub mod autocomplete;`; route link-activation (click) through resolver::resolve_wikilink and, on Unresolved, present the 'Create note' affordance.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/wikilinks/mark.rs (or the MT-015 mark module that owns the rendered link span) — on an Unresolved mark, render the link in the 'unresolved' visual state (Obsidian renders these dimmed/dashed) and attach a click/context action that emits the create-note affordance. After a successful create, mutate the mark to Resolved { document_id } so it re-renders as a live link without a full document reload.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Build ResolverIndex from data already available via the MT-038 Loom search binding. Call GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/search to enumerate candidate documents (title + id). Populate by_title. For aliases: read a per-document `aliases` field if the search/document payload exposes one (it is set via the MT-017 PropertiesPanel). If the field is ABSENT from the backend payload, set ResolverIndex.aliases_supported = false and raise the typed blocker (see step 7).
  2. Resolution order in resolve_wikilink: (a) exact ref/id match, (b) exact title match (case-insensitive, whitespace-normalized), (c) alias match against by_alias. First hit wins; record MatchKind accordingly. No match => Unresolved { title }.
  3. Click activation: when the user clicks a wikilink mark, call resolve_wikilink. If Resolved, route the open through the existing MT-015 link-open path (navigate to document_id). If Unresolved, show a small inline affordance (egui button or popup) labeled 'Create note "{title}"'.
  4. Create-from-unresolved: when the operator confirms, emit EditorIntent::CreateNote { title }. The intent handler calls POST /knowledge/documents (MT-037 binding) with the title and an empty body, receives the new document_id, inserts it into ResolverIndex.by_title, and rewrites the originating mark to Resolved { document_id }. The link must become live immediately (no manual refresh).
  5. Alias autocomplete: while the user types `[[query`, the MT-015 autocomplete trigger calls candidates_for_query. Candidates matched by alias render with a secondary label (e.g. `Project Atlas — alias: "atlas"`). Selecting an alias candidate inserts the canonical `[[Title]]` (or `[[Title|displayed]]` if Obsidian alias-display syntax is in scope per MT-015) that resolves to document_id.
  6. AccessKit: the 'Create note' affordance must register an AccessKit node with a STABLE author_id of the form `wikilink-create-{hash}` where {hash} is a short stable hash of the unresolved title (so swarm agents and kittest can address it deterministically), Role::Button, actions=[Press]. Reuse the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) rather than minting ids ad hoc. Each alias autocomplete row should also carry a stable author_id (e.g. `wikilink-candidate-{document_id}`).
  7. TYPED BLOCKER (aliases gap): if the backend Loom search / knowledge document payload does NOT expose an `aliases` field, do NOT silently no-op. (a) Raise a typed blocker record in the standard WP-012 blocker channel (the packet's typed-gap mechanism) naming the missing field and the two affected APIs; (b) stub alias resolution LOCAL-ONLY: keep a process-local alias map (empty unless populated by the MT-017 PropertiesPanel in-session) so the code path is exercised and testable; (c) render a VISIBLE banner in the rich editor stating alias resolution is running local-only / backend aliases unavailable, so the operator is never misled into thinking aliases are persisted. The CREATE-FROM-UNRESOLVED half is independent of aliases and MUST still proceed fully.
  8. Reuse only — do NOT add SQLite or any local DB. All persistence is PostgreSQL/EventLedger through the existing handshake_core HTTP APIs. The local alias stub in step 7 is in-memory only and is explicitly NOT a persistence layer.

React reference to port: app/src/lib/editor/wikilink.ts holds classifyWikilink + the resolution function (exact vs unresolved classification) — port the classification and resolution branching into resolver.rs. app/src/components/RichDocumentView.tsx holds the link-activation routing (what happens on click for resolved vs unresolved) — port the routing decision into the click handler, but emit a command-bus intent instead of an inline fetch.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-015 (E2-MT015-WikilinksTransclusion) must be merged: provides the wikilink mark, parser, and autocomplete dropdown shell this MT extends.
  - MT-017 (E2-MT017-PropertiesPanel) must be merged: it is where per-document `aliases` are authored; the resolver reads them.
  - MT-037 (E6-MT-037-BindKnowledgeDocumentsApi) must be merged: provides POST /knowledge/documents.
  - MT-038 (E6-MT-038-BindLoomApi) must be merged: provides GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/search for title/alias enumeration.

Interop edge: create-from-link writes through the MT-037 /knowledge/documents binding so the newly created note is immediately addressable as a Loom block (E5 interconnection) with live backlinks — growing the note graph from a single click.

Risk if missed: without create-on-click and alias resolution, the note graph cannot grow from authoring flow and multi-name linking parity with Obsidian is broken; MT-044 (which depends on this) is blocked.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: Clicking an unresolved `[[Title]]` wikilink emits a CreateNote intent carrying the link title — proven by a cargo test that activates an unresolved mark and asserts the emitted EditorIntent::CreateNote { title } matches the link text.

AC-002: After the CreateNote intent resolves (POST /knowledge/documents returns a document_id), the originating mark transitions from Unresolved to Resolved { document_id } and re-renders as a live link without a full document reload — proven by cargo test asserting mark state before/after.
AC-003: resolve_wikilink matches a target by a declared alias (not only exact ref/title) and returns MatchKind::Alias — proven by a cargo test seeding ResolverIndex.by_alias and asserting resolution.

AC-004: An unresolved wikilink renders a visible 'Create note' affordance, and an AccessKit node with author_id of the form 'wikilink-create-{hash}' (Role=Button) is present — proven by a kittest AccessKit tree dump.

AC-005: Alias autocomplete: typing a query that matches a document's alias surfaces that document as a candidate with matched_alias set, rendered with an alias secondary label — proven by cargo test on candidates_for_query plus a kittest dropdown assertion.

AC-006: Missing-aliases-backend path: when the backend payload lacks an `aliases` field, the typed-gap blocker is raised AND a visible local-only banner is shown AND the create-from-unresolved half still works — proven by a cargo test on the missing-aliases path asserting the banner state and that the path does not silently no-op.

AC-007: No SQLite or local DB introduced; all persistence routes through PostgreSQL/EventLedger via existing handshake_core HTTP APIs — proven by grep showing no sqlite/rusqlite usage and that creation calls the MT-037 binding.
AC-008: cargo test -p handshake-native passes with no failures across all wikilink resolver tests.
proof_targets: PT-001: cargo test -p handshake-native wikilink_resolver -- --nocapture  =>  all tests GREEN, no panics
PT-002: cargo test asserting an unresolved-link click emits CreateNote intent and post-create mark becomes Resolved
PT-003: cargo test asserting resolve_wikilink returns MatchKind::Alias for an alias-only target
PT-004: kittest AccessKit tree snapshot asserting a 'wikilink-create-{hash}' Button node is present on an unresolved link
PT-005: cargo test on the missing-aliases backend path asserting the typed-gap banner is shown and the create half still proceeds
implementation_notes: Reuse the WP-011 shell primitives: emit the create-note action through the existing command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) rather than calling fetch inline; pull AccessKit ids through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) rather than minting strings ad hoc.
Resolution normalization must match Obsidian defaults: trim, collapse internal whitespace, case-insensitive title/alias compare. Keep canonical (original-case) display titles for rendering.
candidates_for_query must dedupe by document_id: a doc that matches both by title and by alias appears once, with the higher score; set matched_alias only when the winning match was an alias.

The 'Create note {hash}' author_id hash must be STABLE for a given unresolved title within a session so kittest and swarm agents can target it deterministically; use a short hex hash of the normalized title.

Do NOT call POST /knowledge/documents directly from the egui click handler frame; emit an intent and let the intent handler perform the async call, then mutate the mark on completion to avoid blocking the egui frame thread.
The local-only alias stub (typed-blocker path) is in-memory only; it must be clearly NOT a persistence layer and must not write any file or DB.

Cross-WP dependency: this MT extends the WP-011 native shell — it relies on the existing command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs) and the AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) delivered in WP-KERNEL-011; those modules already exist under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/ and are reused, not rebuilt.

React reference shapes: app/src/lib/editor/wikilink.ts classifyWikilink/resolution branching maps to resolver.rs; app/src/components/RichDocumentView.tsx link-activation routing maps to the click handler (but emit an intent, not an inline fetch).

PATH + EXTEND-NOT-CREATE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-24, verified Get-ChildItem): rich_text/ -> rich_editor/ (the real dir). CRITICAL: rich_editor/wikilinks/autocomplete.rs ALREADY EXISTS (MT-015 built it) - EXTEND it with the alias-aware candidate path, do NOT create a 2nd. mark.rs is FICTIONAL - the MT-015 wikilink render/unresolved-state lives in inline_view.rs (+ confirm.rs may already host a create-confirmation affordance, runtime.rs the resolver/runtime state). resolver.rs is genuinely NEW. allowed_paths corrected: resolver.rs (new) + autocomplete.rs (EXTEND) + inline_view.rs (unresolved render + create affordance) + confirm.rs + runtime.rs (resolver index) + mod.rs + test. REUSE the MT-015 mark/parser/autocomplete-dropdown shell - this MT adds the richer resolver + alias-aware candidates feeding the EXISTING dropdown; do NOT re-implement the mark/[[..]] parser/dropdown.

ALIASES GAP = TYPED BLOCKER + LOCAL STUB + BANNER (KERNEL_BUILDER gate - the backend has NO aliases field, grep-confirmed): VERIFY whether the GET /loom/graph-search (MT-038/029 verified route - the contract's /loom/search) or the knowledge-document payload exposes an `aliases` field (set via MT-017 PropertiesPanel). The initial signal is it is ABSENT. If absent (AC-006/RISK-002/MC-002): (a) raise the typed-gap blocker naming the missing field + the 2 affected APIs; (b) keep a PROCESS-LOCAL in-memory alias map (in-session only, NOT a persistence layer, NO SQLite/file - AC-007/MC-006) so the alias code path is exercised + testable; (c) render a VISIBLE banner in the rich editor 'alias resolution local-only / backend aliases unavailable' so the operator is NEVER misled. NEVER a silent no-op. The CREATE-FROM-UNRESOLVED half is INDEPENDENT of aliases + MUST fully proceed.

CREATE-VIA-INTENT + MARK REWRITE + RESOLUTION ORDER (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): create-from-unresolved emits a COMMAND-BUS intent (EditorIntent::CreateNote via command_registry+event_bus) - NEVER an inline POST on the egui click frame (RISK-007/MC-007 - frame freeze); the intent handler async-calls POST /knowledge/documents (MT-037 create_document binding - the VERIFIED route, NOT a new endpoint), gets the document_id, inserts into ResolverIndex.by_title, and REWRITES the originating mark Unresolved->Resolved{document_id} so the link goes LIVE without a reload (RISK-003/MC-003, AC-002). DEBOUNCE/guard the affordance (in-flight flag keyed on normalized title) so a double-click does NOT POST twice = duplicate notes (RISK-001/MC-001). resolve_wikilink ORDER: exact ref/id -> exact title (case-insensitive, whitespace-normalized) -> alias; first hit wins; alias-collides-with-title -> EXACT TITLE WINS, deterministic + unit-tested (RISK-004/MC-004). candidates_for_query dedupes by document_id (higher score; matched_alias only when alias won). AccessKit wikilink-create-{stable-hex-hash-of-normalized-title} (Button) + wikilink-candidate-{document_id} via accessibility/registry.rs - stable hash unit-tested (RISK-005/MC-005). NO SQLite (grep-gate). Screenshot EXTERNAL; full suite -j 2; clippy clean.

HARDEN RESOLUTION (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-24 - the panel caught UNWIRED SCAFFOLDING, 6 must-fixes all one root; the auto-harden died mid-wiring, partial edits discarded): the impl (513f2869) shipped SOLID PURE LOGIC (resolver/candidates_for_query/confirm) but NONE of it is wired into the LIVE editor - candidates_for_query has 0 production callers (the live render_autocomplete_popup renders only backend rows), CreateNoteBackend never installed (create POST never fires), ResolverIndex never seeded from Loom search (every [[Title]] is Unresolved at runtime), banner dead, AC-005 test is a standalone-egui-list tautology. RESOLUTION (wire it + re-prove against the REAL widget, gate live-backend e2e): (1) WIRE candidates_for_query(&resolver_index,query) into RichEditorWidget::render_autocomplete_popup - merge alias candidates into the LIVE dropdown rows + render the matched_alias secondary label + assign candidate_author_id(document_id); DELETE the standalone-list AC-005 tautology + re-prove against the LIVE popup (open [[ trigger, type an alias query, assert a wikilink-candidate-{id} row with the alias label in render_autocomplete_popup). (2) WIRE set_wikilink_context (the production hook) to set_create_backend(KnowledgeCreateNoteBackend::production) + SEED resolver_index by calling the Loom search client (client.rs LoomSearchV2 / verified /loom/graph-search) to enumerate titles into by_title on mount/refresh + note_alias_backend_gap() (backend has no aliases field, so the banner reflects real state); route MT-017 alias authoring through add_local_alias. Re-prove AC-002/003/006 against the real widget (set_wikilink_context-installed backend + spy capturing the POST shape; seeded index -> resolution works; banner shown). (3) The live end-to-end (real Loom-search seed + real create POST against managed PostgreSQL) = NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF; the shell PaneFactory CALLING set_wikilink_context on the mounted editor = E11/MT-069 (carry). Keep full suite green (-j 2; test_e7_swarm_edit_proof + test_embeds wgpu-parallelism segfaults are PRE-EXISTING + unrelated to MT-057 - confirm those bins green in isolation).
ports_from_react: app/src/lib/editor/wikilink.ts (classifyWikilink, resolution) — port the resolved-vs-unresolved classification and the resolution branching into src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/wikilinks/resolver.rs
app/src/components/RichDocumentView.tsx (link activation routing) — port the click-routing decision for resolved vs unresolved links into the egui click handler
binds_backend_api: POST /knowledge/documents (create note) — via the MT-037 binding; used by the create-from-unresolved-link path
GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/search (alias/title resolution) — via the MT-038 binding; used to build ResolverIndex (title + alias enumeration)

risks: RISK-001: Create-from-unresolved races: rapid double-click could POST /knowledge/documents twice and create duplicate notes for the same title.
RISK-002: The aliases backend gap is silently swallowed — alias resolution returns no matches and the operator wrongly believes aliases are unsupported or broken, with no typed blocker and no banner.
RISK-003: After create, the originating mark is not rewritten to Resolved, so the link stays visually unresolved until a full document reload, breaking the immediate-live-link expectation.
RISK-004: Alias and title collisions: an alias on doc A equals a title on doc B; ambiguous resolution silently picks the wrong document.
RISK-005: AccessKit author_id collisions when multiple unresolved links share the same title, or non-stable hashes break kittest/swarm targeting.

RISK-006: Accidental backend rewrite or a new endpoint is added instead of binding the existing MT-037/MT-038 APIs, or SQLite is introduced for the alias stub, violating the PostgreSQL/EventLedger-only and no-backend-rewrite constraints.
RISK-007: Network call performed inline on the egui click frame blocks the UI thread and freezes the editor during note creation.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Debounce/guard create-from-unresolved: disable the affordance and mark an in-flight state once CreateNote is emitted so a second click cannot fire a duplicate POST; key the guard on the normalized title.
MC-002: The missing-aliases path MUST raise the typed-gap blocker AND render the visible local-only banner — assert both in a test; never allow a silent no-op (enforced by AC-006).
MC-003: Post-create mark rewrite to Resolved { document_id } is covered by AC-002 and a regression test; the link must re-render live without reload.
MC-004: Resolution order is deterministic and documented (ref, then exact title, then alias); when an alias collides with a title, exact title wins and the decision is unit-tested.

MC-005: Use the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) and a stable short hash of the normalized title for 'wikilink-create-{hash}'; unit-test hash stability and uniqueness across distinct titles.
MC-006: grep-gate proving no rusqlite/sqlite usage and that creation routes through the MT-037 binding (not a new endpoint) and resolution through the MT-038 binding (enforced by AC-007).
MC-007: All network I/O for create routes through an emitted intent + async handler, never inline on the egui frame thread (enforced by implementation note and reviewed in proof).

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + real rich_editor/wikilinks/ (autocomplete.rs EXISTS, inline_view.rs not mark.rs, confirm.rs/runtime.rs) + MT-015 engine + MT-037 create_document + MT-038/029 graph-search + aliases-field absent (grep)
corrections: rich_text/->rich_editor/; autocomplete.rs EXISTS (extend not create); mark.rs fictional -> inline_view.rs; resolver.rs new; reuse MT-015 mark/parser/dropdown
aliases field ABSENT in backend (grep) -> typed blocker + in-memory local stub + visible banner (no silent no-op, no SQLite); create-half independent + must work
create via command-bus intent (not inline POST, debounced) + mark rewrite Unresolved->Resolved (live, no reload); POST /knowledge/documents = MT-037 verified route
resolution order ref->title->alias (title wins collision); dedupe by doc_id; accesskit stable-hash create id; no SQLite; -j 2 full suite

kernel_builder_harden_resolution.root_cause: UNWIRED SCAFFOLDING (MT-035/008/010/014 class) - resolver/candidates/create-backend/banner all dead at runtime; AC-005 test tautological; auto-harden died mid-wiring (partial edits discarded).
kernel_builder_harden_resolution.fix: wire candidates_for_query->live render_autocomplete_popup + set_create_backend + seed resolver_index from Loom search + flip banner via set_wikilink_context; re-prove against the REAL widget.
kernel_builder_harden_resolution.gated: live Loom-search seed + real create POST = NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF; shell calling set_wikilink_context on the mounted editor = E11/MT-069.

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_7c3d19d3 (impl + 3-lens panel) + re-harden agent a83d6fb2 (the auto-harden died mid-wiring; re-run honestly)

31f62d3b fix harden - wire alias autocomplete + create backend + resolver-index seed + banner into the live editor
implementation_result.review_panel: correctness=CHANGES_REQUIRED(2), backend-shape=CHANGES_REQUIRED(4), perf-focus-hygiene=PASS(0) -> 6 must-fixes, ALL one root: UNWIRED SCAFFOLDING (the 2nd such catch after MT-035; MT-008/010/014 class). The auto-harden died mid-wiring (partial edits discarded); KERNEL_BUILDER re-ran a focused coder-protocol harden.
implementation_result.scaffolding_fixed_VERIFIED: INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED the wiring is REAL: render_autocomplete_popup:2885 now calls candidates_for_query (alias rows in the LIVE dropdown + matched_alias secondary label + wikilink-candidate-{id}); set_wikilink_context:317/326/333 installs set_create_backend(KnowledgeCreateNoteBackend::production) + seed_resolver_index_from_search (POST /loom/search-v2 enumerate titles -> resolver_index) + note_alias_backend_gap (banner reflects real no-aliases backend). The AC-005 standalone-list TAUTOLOGY deleted -> ac005_alias_candidate_dropdown_row_live_widget (mounts RichEditorWidget, types [[qp, asserts the LIVE render_autocomplete_popup produced wikilink-candidate-DOC-9 with alias label). AC-002 via SpyCreateBackend capturing the POST shape; AC-003 via seeded-index resolution; AC-006 via the production hook banner.
implementation_result.proofs: 82 lib wikilink + 12 test_wikilink_resolver + regression (theme 19 incl CONTROL-4, accesskit 9, rich_widget 6, wikilinks 8) GREEN via -j 2; test_embeds 6 + test_e7_swarm_edit_proof 5 pass in ISOLATION (the bare full-parallel suite is flaky from PRE-EXISTING wgpu-parallelism segfaults in those 2 unrelated bins, NOT MT-057); clippy clean; create-affordance + alias-candidate-row visually verified; screenshots EXTERNAL.
implementation_result.gated_and_carried: live end-to-end (real Loom-search seed + real POST /knowledge/documents vs managed PostgreSQL) = NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF (live_pg_set_wikilink_context_seeds_and_creates #[ignore]+integration). The shell PaneFactory CALLING set_wikilink_context on the mounted editor = E11/MT-069 carry-forward (wiring proven at the widget level, not faked).
implementation_result.strategic_gap: ALIASES BACKEND GAP (verified, like the Flight Recorder gap): the backend Loom-search + knowledge-document payloads have NO `aliases` field. Native editor uses an in-memory in-session local-alias stub + a VISIBLE local-only banner + a typed blocker. FULL alias persistence needs a future BACKEND packet (out of this frozen-backend WP).
implementation_result.waiver_granted: rich_editor/wikilinks/resolver.rs (new) + autocomplete.rs/runtime.rs/confirm.rs/inline_view.rs/mod.rs (extend) + renderer/rich_editor_widget.rs (live wiring) + test in allowed_paths.
